doc.title: Lease Classification — The Five Tests
doc.event: Tatum Co. signs an equipment lease: no ownership transfer, no purchase option, a 6-year term on equipment with a 10-year economic life, PV of lease payments of $442,000 on equipment with a $480,000 fair value, and the asset is not specialized. Classify the lease, supporting your answer with the two quantitative tests.

computationPaths.narration: Finance classification requires meeting ANY ONE of five criteria. Ownership, purchase option, and specialized asset all fail here — so it comes down to the two ratio tests.

memorize.body: OWNES — finance if ANY one hits: Ownership transfers · Written purchase option (reasonably certain) · Ninety percent of fair value (PV test) · Economic life 75% (term test) · Specialized asset.
Meet ONE criterion → finance lease. Meet NONE → operating. There is no scoring — it's a single tripwire.
Don't swap the thresholds: 75% goes with LIFE, 90% goes with VALUE.

questions.prompt: Equipment fair value $480,000. What is the minimum PV of lease payments that triggers finance classification under the fair-value test?

distractors.feedback: You used 75% — that threshold belongs to the economic-LIFE test. The fair-value test uses 90%: 0.90 × 480,000 = 432,000.
That's the actual PV in this problem. The question asked for the 90% THRESHOLD: 432,000.
